previous_statement: (Well-definedness of the map — CORRECTED). The map Phi : {T in pi_phi(A)'^+ : <T xi_phi, xi_phi> = 1} -> S(A) defined by Phi(T) = omega_T where omega_T(a) = <pi_phi(a) T xi_phi, xi_phi> is well-defined: omega_T is a state on A. Proof: (i) Linearity of omega_T in a: clear from linearity of pi_phi and of the inner product. (ii) Positivity: Since pi_phi is a *-homomorphism, pi_phi(a*a) = pi_phi(a)* pi_phi(a). Since T commutes with pi_phi(a) (as T in pi_phi(A)'), we have pi_phi(a) T = T pi_phi(a). Therefore: omega_T(a*a) = <pi_phi(a)* pi_phi(a) T xi_phi, xi_phi> = <pi_phi(a)* T pi_phi(a) xi_phi, xi_phi> = <T pi_phi(a) xi_phi, pi_phi(a) xi_phi> >= 0, where the last step uses T >= 0 (i.e., <T eta, eta> >= 0 for all eta). (iii) Normalisation: omega_T(1) = <pi_phi(1) T xi_phi, xi_phi> = <T xi_phi, xi_phi> = 1 by the domain constraint on T.
new_statement:
(Well-definedness of the map — 2nd amendment, cleaned). The map Φ : {T ∈ π_φ(A)'^+ : ⟨Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = 1} → S(A) defined by Φ(T) = ω_T where ω_T(a) = ⟨π_φ(a)Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ is well-defined: ω_T is a state on A.

Proof: (i) Linearity in a: clear from linearity of π_φ and linearity of the inner product in its first argument.

(ii) Positivity: ω_T(a*a) = ⟨π_φ(a*a)Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = ⟨π_φ(a)*π_φ(a)Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ (since π_φ is a *-homomorphism). Since T ∈ π_φ(A)', we have Tπ_φ(a) = π_φ(a)T, hence π_φ(a)T = Tπ_φ(a), giving ⟨π_φ(a)*Tπ_φ(a)ξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = ⟨Tπ_φ(a)ξ_φ, π_φ(a)ξ_φ⟩ ≥ 0, where the last step uses T ≥ 0 (⟨Tη, η⟩ ≥ 0 for all η, with η = π_φ(a)ξ_φ).

(iii) Normalisation: ω_T(1) = ⟨π_φ(1)Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = ⟨Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = 1 by the domain constraint. This is a direct proof, not an assumption.
